title: Project legacy ledger and audit views from Event Ledger
description: Refactor read-ledger and audit-log services so public callers can read compatible LedgerEntry and AuditLogEntry views from the unified Event Ledger while preserving legacy fallback reads during migration. Update /api/ledger, doctor audit window matching, history replay, rehydrate state, and SSE event watching to include .fabric/events.jsonl.
test: Tests for readLedger projection, readAuditLog projection/window matching, legacy fallback, /api/ledger compatibility, and /events watching events.jsonl.
acceptance_criteria: Existing dashboard/history/doctor consumers still receive compatible data; audit compliance works from event records; legacy .intent-ledger.jsonl and audit.jsonl can still be read as fallback; SSE reacts to .fabric/events.jsonl.
hints: Build on T1 event reader/writer; preserve existing API payloads in this task || packages/server/src/services/read-ledger.ts;packages/server/src/services/audit-log.ts;packages/server/src/api/events.ts;packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts
execution_directives: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test -- read-ledger audit-log doctor index --runInBand
prev_context:
[Explore event-ledger-storage] Current storage is dual JSONL: .fabric/.intent-ledger.jsonl plus legacy fallback for LedgerEntry, and .fabric/audit.jsonl for get_rules/edit_intent/rule_selection. HTTP MCP EventStore also writes kind=mcp-event lines into the ledger file. /api/ledger and history replay consume readLedger; /events watches ledger/meta/lock/forensic, not audit. Migration needs .fabric/events.jsonl plus compatibility projections for ledger views, audit compliance windows, MCP replay, and SSE semantics.
